title: hieracium vulgatum subsp. tersiflorum
intro: Hieracium vulgatum subsp. tersiflorum, also known as the common hawkweed or lesser hawkbit, is a perennial plant belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful in treating wounds, infections, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Hieracium vulgatum subsp. tersiflorum include its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with gastrointestinal disorders, as well as its ability to boost the immune system. The herbal preparations of this plant can be found in the form of teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are typically consumed to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
